Off Calendar Jul-19-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. ANNETTE D. KIRKHAM, Esq. of Department of Industrial RElations - Division of Labor Standards Enforcement (Tel: 415-792-3029) for Plaintiff/Respondent Annettte Lorenzo (via Zoom videoconference;
Attys. SARA ALEXIS LEVINE ABARBANEL, Esq. (Tel: 858-847-6700) and EVAN L. HAMLING, Esq. (Tel: 415-434-4484) of Foley & Lardner LLP for Defendants/Appellants San Francisco Zen Center, Linda A. Galijan, and Mike Smith.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO ZEN CENTER, LINDA A. GALIJAN, and MIKE SMITH's Motion To File Under Seal.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to seal is denied without prejudice.

To enable a court to make the specific findings required by California Rule of Court 2.550(d), a party moving to seal court records must present "specific enumeration of the facts sought to be withheld and specific reasons for withholding them." (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.) Here, while exhibits are listed, the specific facts sought to be withheld are not enumerated. Moreover, while broad reasons for withholding documents (e.g., "sensitive, confidential") are also listed, little or no attempt is made to link facts with reasons.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: S. Kane; Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR No. 11975, Email: csrjane@hotmail.com, Tel: 909-831-2644 (302/RCE)

Plaintiff's "motion to dismiss defendants Mike Smith and Linda Galijan's appeal of the order, decision, or award [OAD] of the labor commissioner for failure to post undertaking mandated by Labor Code 98.2(b)" is denied.

Plaintiff claimed unpaid employment by the San Francisco Zen Center, of which Smith and Galijan were managers. The September 6, 2022 OAD found for plaintiff in the amount of $149,177.15 against these three defendants, stating: "but Plaintiff can only recover $149,177.15 from all Defendants together." (Id. at 16:13.) Later in September 2022, all three defendants jointly appealed the OAD to this court and the Zen Center posted a Labor Code 98.2 undertaking of $149,177.15.

Fifteen months later, plaintiff now seeks to dismiss Smith and Galijan's portion of the joint appeal because they too did not each post a $149,177.15 Labor Code 98.2 undertaking, thus tripling the amount the OAD found plaintiff to be owed. This is not a reasonable reading of the law. Labor Code 98.2(b) provides: "As a condition to filing an appeal pursuant to this section, an employer shall first post an undertaking with the reviewing court in the amount of the order, decision, or award." Plaintiff's putative employer has posted that undertaking.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
